attempted_route: Runtime pip install dgl from data.dgl.ai wheels; on failure fell back to dc.models.MPNNModel(n_tasks=1, mode='regression').fit(NumpyDataset of GraphData objects, nb_epoch=50) + np.savetxt
observation: dgl wheel unavailable for Python 3.13 (pip install fails). MPNNModel fallback crashes at fit() with AttributeError: 'GraphData' object has no attribute 'get_num_atoms' in graph_models.py line 1144
rationale: dgl has no Python 3.13 wheel so runtime install cannot fix CGCNNModel. MPNNModel fallback also fails because perovskite X is an array of GraphData objects; MPNNModel.default_generator calls mol.get_num_atoms() which GraphData does not implement. Both graph model paths are blocked.
recommended_alternative: Use dc.models.AttentiveFPModel(n_tasks=1, mode='regression') which is torch-geometric-based and accepts GraphData directly via dc.feat.MolGraphConvFeaturizer output. Alternatively, extract flat node-feature arrays from GraphData.node_features and train dc.models.MultitaskRegressor or dc.models.RobustMultitaskRegressor on the flat arrays.
